headline: Qualcomm Jumps 8%, Micron Pops 4% as AI Semiconductor Rally Enters Parabolic Phase
summary: Shares of Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) are up roughly 8% to around $237 in early Monday trading, extending one of the most aggressive multi-week runs in large-cap semis. Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) is climbing alongside it, up about 4% to around $774. The moves follow a blistering Friday session, when QCOM closed up 8% at $219.09 and MU ... headline: Qualcomm’s 70% Rally May Be Just the Start
summary: While Qualcomm (QCOM) stock has largely been left behind in the AI-driven semiconductor rally over the last few years, the tide appears to have turned for the company, with the stock up by close to 70% over the past month as Qualcomm is positioning for the next phase of AI, one that moves beyond centralized compute and into billions of connected devices. headline: Memory chip stocks hit record highs, pharma reacts to hantavirus concerns
summary: Market Catalysts Host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma track several of the day's top trending stock tickers, including memory chip stocks Micron Technology (MU), Intel (INTC), and Qualcomm (QCOM) opening at record highs; shares of Moderna (MRNA) and Novavax (NVAX) reacting to concerns around the hantavirus; and Constellation Energy (CEG) affirming its full-year guidance after beating first quarter earnings estimates.

headline: S&P 500 Heads for All-Time High Lifted by Energy Sector
summary: The S&P 500 is up by a modest amount, but if it keeps pace it will mark its 16th record close of 2026.  The index tracking 500 of the largest U.S. publicly traded companies was up 0.2%.  Oil prices are rising after President Trump slammed Iran's response to a U.S. peace proposal.

headline: Elon Musk, Apple's Cook and Boeing CEO going to China with Trump, official says
summary: Elon Musk, Apple's Tim Cook, GE Aerospace's Larry Culp and ‌Boeing's Kelly Ortberg will join U.S. President Donald Trump on his visit ‌to China this week, a White House official told Reuters.  Others taking part include Meta's Dina ​Powell McCormick, Blackrock's Larry Fink, Blackstone's Stephen Schwarzman, Cisco's Chuck Robbins, Micron's Sanjay Mehrota, Mastercard'ss Michael Miebach, Qualcomm's Christiano Amon and Visa's Ryan McInerney, the official said.

content.title: Elon Musk, Apple's Cook and Boeing CEO going to China with Trump, official says
content.summary: Elon Musk, Apple's Tim Cook, GE Aerospace's Larry Culp and ‌Boeing's Kelly Ortberg will join U.S. President Donald Trump on his visit ‌to China this week, a White House official told Reuters.  Others taking part include Meta's Dina ​Powell McCormick, Blackrock's Larry Fink, Blackstone's Stephen Schwarzman, Cisco's Chuck Robbins, Micron's Sanjay Mehrota, Mastercard'ss Michael Miebach, Qualcomm's Christiano Amon and Visa's Ryan McInerney, the official said.
